passage_title: What's the purpose of gaining knowledge?

sentences.sentence: 'I would found an institution where any person can find instruction in any subject.'
sentences.translation: “我要创办一所任何人都能在任何学科中获得教育的机构。”

structure.text: I would found an institution
where any person can find instruction in any subject

difficult_points: would found 表达的是创始人立志办学时的意愿，而非单纯的过去时态叙述。
where 引导定语从句修饰 institution，说明这所机构的性质。

sentences.sentence: That was the founder's motto for Cornell University, and it seems an apt characterization of the different university, also in the USA, where I currently teach philosophy.
sentences.translation: 这是康奈尔大学创始人的座右铭，而它似乎也恰好适用于我目前任教哲学的另一所大学——同样也在美国。

structure.text: That
was the founder's motto for Cornell University
and it seems an apt characterization of the different university, also in the USA, where I currently teach philosophy
also in the USA
where I currently teach philosophy

difficult_points: That 指代前一句中康奈尔大学创始人所说的那句座右铭，作本句主语。
the different university 指“另一所（不同于康奈尔的）大学”，即作者目前任教的学校。
also in the USA 是插入的补充说明，指出这所大学同样位于美国。
where 引导定语从句修饰 university，说明作者在那里教授哲学。

sentences.sentence: A student can prepare for a career in resort management, engineering, interior design, accounting, music, law enforcement, you name it.
sentences.translation: 学生可以为从事度假村管理、工程、室内设计、会计、音乐、执法等各种职业做准备——无论什么职业，应有尽有。

structure.text: A student
can prepare for a career in resort management, engineering, interior design, accounting, music, law enforcement
you name it

difficult_points: prepare for a career in 后接一连串并列名词，列举可供选择的职业方向。
you name it 是口语习语，意为“无论什么，你能想到的都有”，用于强调职业种类繁多。

expressions.meaning_zh: 应有尽有；你能想到的都有

sentences.sentence: But what would the founders of these two institutions have thought of a course called 'Arson for Profit'?
sentences.translation: 但这两所学校的创始人若是看到一门名为“以纵火牟利”的课程，会作何感想呢？

structure.text: But
what would the founders of these two institutions have thought of a course called 'Arson for Profit'
the founders of these two institutions
called 'Arson for Profit'

difficult_points: But 是句首转折连接词，引出下面的反问。
would...have thought of 是虚拟语气的过去将来完成时，表达对假设情境的推测。
called 'Arson for Profit' 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 course，说明课程的名称。

sentences.sentence: I kid you not: we have it on the books.
sentences.translation: 我没有开玩笑：这门课确实列在我们的课程目录里。

structure.text: I kid you not
we have it on the books

difficult_points: kid you not 是口语化表达，意为“没有开玩笑，说的是真的”。
on the books 指“正式列入名册/课程目录”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 正式列入名册/记录在案

sentences.sentence: Any undergraduates who have met the academic requirements can sign up for the course in our program in 'fire science'.
sentences.translation: 任何达到学术要求的本科生，都可以报名参加我们“火灾科学”专业中的这门课程。

structure.text: Any undergraduates
who have met the academic requirements
can sign up for the course in our program in 'fire science'

difficult_points: who 引导定语从句修饰 undergraduates，限定符合条件的学生范围。
have met 是现在完成时，强调“已经达到”这一既成状态。

sentences.sentence: Naturally, the course is intended for prospective arson investigators, who can learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law.
sentences.translation: 自然，这门课程是为未来的纵火调查员开设的，他们能学到判断火灾是否人为纵火、查出纵火者，以及建立证据链以便在法庭上有效起诉的各种专业技巧。

structure.text: Naturally
the course is intended for prospective arson investigators
who can learn all the tricks of the trade for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law
for detecting whether a fire was deliberately set, discovering who did it, and establishing a chain of evidence for effective prosecution in a court of law
whether a fire was deliberately set
who did it

difficult_points: who 引导定语从句修饰 investigators，说明他们能学到的具体技能。
for detecting..., discovering..., and establishing... 是三个并列的动名词短语，共同说明 tricks of the trade 的具体内容。
whether 引导的从句作 detecting 的宾语，who did it 作 discovering 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: But wouldn't this also be the perfect course for prospective arsonists to sign up for?
sentences.translation: 但这难道不也正是潜在纵火犯会想报名参加的完美课程吗？

structure.text: But
wouldn't this also be the perfect course for prospective arsonists to sign up for
for prospective arsonists to sign up for

difficult_points: wouldn't...be 是否定疑问句，表达反问语气，暗示答案应是肯定的。
for prospective arsonists to sign up for 是“for + 逻辑主语 + 不定式”结构，修饰 course，句末 for 与 sign up for 呼应。

sentences.sentence: My point is not to criticize academic programs in fire science: they are highly welcome as part of the increasing professionalization of this and many other occupations.
sentences.translation: 我的意思并不是要批评火灾科学的学术项目：随着这一职业及其他许多职业日益专业化，这类项目非常受欢迎。

structure.text: My point is not to criticize academic programs in fire science
to criticize academic programs in fire science
they are highly welcome as part of the increasing professionalization of this and many other occupations

difficult_points: My point is not to... 表示“我的用意不是……”，to criticize 是不定式作表语。
as part of 引出背景说明，指这类项目是相关职业专业化进程的一部分。

sentences.sentence: However, it's not unknown for a firefighter to torch a building.
sentences.translation: 然而，消防员纵火烧毁建筑物的事也并非闻所未闻。

structure.text: However
it's not unknown for a firefighter to torch a building
for a firefighter to torch a building

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，真正的逻辑主语是后面 for a firefighter to torch a building 这一不定式结构。
not unknown 是双重否定，表示“并非罕见，确实发生过”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 并非罕见；并不是没有过

sentences.sentence: This example suggests how dishonest and illegal behavior, with the help of higher education, can creep into every aspect of public and business life.
sentences.translation: 这个例子表明，不诚实和违法行为借助高等教育，能够悄悄渗透到公共和商业生活的方方面面。

structure.text: This example
suggests
how dishonest and illegal behavior, with the help of higher education, can creep into every aspect of public and business life
with the help of higher education
can creep into every aspect of public and business life

difficult_points: how 引导宾语从句，作 suggests 的宾语。
with the help of higher education 是插入语，位于从句主谓之间，说明渗透的途径。
creep into 意为“悄悄渗入”，强调这种渗透是逐渐且不易察觉的。

sentences.sentence: I realized this anew when I was invited to speak before a class in marketing, which is another of our degree programs.
sentences.translation: 当我受邀在一堂市场营销课上发言时，我再次意识到了这一点——市场营销也是我们的另一个学位项目。

structure.text: I realized this anew
when I was invited to speak before a class in marketing
to speak before a class in marketing
which is another of our degree programs

difficult_points: this 指代上一段所说的“不诚实行为借助高等教育渗透到公共和商业生活”这一现象。
when 引导时间状语从句，说明再次意识到这一点的具体情境。
which 引导非限定性定语从句，补充说明 marketing 是学校的另一门学位课程。

sentences.sentence: The regular instructor is a colleague who appreciates the kind of ethical perspective I can bring as a philosopher.
sentences.translation: 这门课的常任授课老师是我的一位同事，他很欣赏我作为哲学家所能带来的那种伦理视角。

structure.text: The regular instructor
is a colleague
who appreciates the kind of ethical perspective I can bring as a philosopher
I can bring as a philosopher

difficult_points: who 引导定语从句修饰 colleague，说明这位同事的态度。
I can bring as a philosopher 是省略了关系代词的定语从句，修饰 perspective。

sentences.sentence: There are endless ways I could have approached this assignment, but I took my cue from the title of the course: 'Principles of Marketing'.
sentences.translation: 这项任务本可以有很多种处理方式，但我是从这门课的名字——“市场营销原理”——中获得的灵感。

structure.text: There are endless ways
I could have approached this assignment
but I took my cue from the title of the course: 'Principles of Marketing'

difficult_points: I could have approached this assignment 省略了关系词，修饰 ways，could have done 表示“本可以做但实际未必如此”的虚拟语气。
take one's cue from 意为“从……中获得启发/线索”。

sentences.sentence: It made me think to ask the students, 'Is marketing principled?'
sentences.translation: 这让我想到要去问学生们：“市场营销讲究原则吗？”

structure.text: It
made me think
to ask the students, 'Is marketing principled?'

difficult_points: made me think 是“make + 宾语 + 原形动词”的使役结构。
to ask the students... 是不定式，表示“想到就去问”的结果，相当于 think to do（想着去做）。
principled 是形容词，意为“讲原则的、合乎道德规范的”，呼应下文对该词双重含义的讨论。

sentences.sentence: After all, a subject matter can have principles in the sense of being codified, having rules, as with football or chess, without being principled in the sense of being ethical.
sentences.translation: 毕竟，一门学科可以在“已被编纂成规则、有章可循”的意义上拥有原则（就像足球或国际象棋那样），却未必在“合乎道德”的意义上讲原则。

structure.text: After all
a subject matter can have principles
in the sense of being codified, having rules, as with football or chess
as with football or chess
without being principled in the sense of being ethical

difficult_points: in the sense of 表示“在……意义上”，句中两次出现，分别对应 principles 的两种不同含义。
being codified, having rules 是两个动名词短语并列，说明“有原则”的一种含义。
as with football or chess 是插入的类比，举例说明规则化的学科。
without 引导的介词短语与前面的 have principles 形成对比，指出规则化不等于道德化。

sentences.sentence: Many of the students immediately assumed that the answer to my question about marketing principles was obvious: no.
sentences.translation: 许多学生立刻认定，我提出的关于市场营销原则的问题，其答案显而易见：不讲原则。

structure.text: Many of the students
immediately assumed
that the answer to my question about marketing principles was obvious
no

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，作 assumed 的宾语。
冒号后的 no 是对 obvious 具体内容的补充说明，即“答案是‘不’”。

sentences.sentence: Just look at the ways in which everything under the sun has been marketed; obviously it need not be done in a principled (=ethical) fashion.
sentences.translation: 只要看看世间万物是如何被营销的就知道了；显然，营销并不必须以讲原则（即符合道德）的方式进行。

structure.text: Just look at the ways
in which everything under the sun has been marketed
obviously it need not be done in a principled (=ethical) fashion

difficult_points: look at 是祈使句，用于引导读者自行验证这一说法。
in which 引导定语从句修饰 ways，被动语态 has been marketed 强调营销这一动作的普遍性。
everything under the sun 是习语，意为“世间万物，一切事物”。
(=ethical) 是作者对 principled 一词的解释性插注。

sentences.sentence: Is that obvious?
sentences.translation: 这难道是显而易见的吗？

structure.text: Is that obvious?

difficult_points: 本句是一般疑问句形式的反问，直接照应上文学生所说的“obviously”（显而易见）。
that 指代前一段学生们“市场营销无需讲原则”这一判断。

sentences.sentence: I made the suggestion, which may sound downright crazy in light of the evidence, that perhaps marketing is by definition principled.
sentences.translation: 我提出了一个设想——这个设想根据现有证据来看，也许显得相当荒唐——即市场营销按定义本身就是讲原则的。

structure.text: I made the suggestion
which may sound downright crazy in light of the evidence
that perhaps marketing is by definition principled

difficult_points: which 引导非限定性定语从句，插入在 suggestion 和 that 从句之间，对整个提议发表评论。
that 引导的从句是 suggestion 的同位语从句，说明设想的具体内容。
by definition 意为“根据定义、从本质上说”，强调这是本质属性而非偶然现象。

sentences.sentence: My inspiration for this judgement is the philosopher Immanuel Kant, who argued that any body of knowledge consists of an end (or purpose) and a means.
sentences.translation: 促使我作出这一判断的灵感来自哲学家伊曼努尔·康德，他认为任何一门知识体系都由目的（或宗旨）与手段构成。

structure.text: My inspiration for this judgement
is the philosopher Immanuel Kant
who argued that any body of knowledge consists of an end (or purpose) and a means
that any body of knowledge consists of an end (or purpose) and a means

difficult_points: who 引导定语从句修饰 Immanuel Kant，说明其观点。
that 引导 argued 的宾语从句。
(or purpose) 是插入的同义解释，说明 end 在此处的含义是“目的”。

sentences.sentence: Let us apply both the terms 'means' and 'end' to marketing.
sentences.translation: 让我们把“手段”和“目的”这两个术语都应用到市场营销上。

structure.text: Let us apply
both the terms 'means' and 'end'
to marketing

difficult_points: Let us + 动词原形是提议性祈使句，意为“让我们……”。
both...and... 强调手段和目的这两个术语都要纳入考虑。

sentences.sentence: The students have signed up for a course in order to learn how to market effectively.
sentences.translation: 学生们报名参加这门课程，是为了学习如何有效地进行市场营销。

structure.text: The students
have signed up for a course
in order to learn how to market effectively
how to market effectively

difficult_points: in order to 表示目的，引出学生报名的原因。
how to market effectively 是 learn 的宾语，由“how + 不定式”构成的名词性结构。

sentences.sentence: But to what end?
sentences.translation: 但目的又是什么呢？

structure.text: But
to what end?

difficult_points: 本句是省略句，完整形式接近 to what end do they do so，意为“目的何在”。
end 在此处是名词，意为“目的”，呼应上文康德关于 end 和 means 的论述。

sentences.sentence: There seem to be two main attitudes toward that question.
sentences.translation: 对于这个问题，似乎存在两种主要态度。

structure.text: There seem to be two main attitudes
toward that question

difficult_points: There seem to be 是存在句结构，seem 表推测语气。
that question 指代前一句提出的“目的是什么”这一问题。

sentences.sentence: One is that the answer is obvious: the purpose of marketing is to sell things and to make money.
sentences.translation: 第一种态度认为，答案显而易见：市场营销的目的就是销售产品、赚取利润。

structure.text: One
is
that the answer is obvious
the purpose of marketing is to sell things and to make money

difficult_points: One 指代前一句提到的“两种主要态度”之一。
that 引导表语从句，说明 One 具体所指的内容。
冒号后的内容具体说明 obvious 所指的答案是什么。

sentences.sentence: The other attitude is that the purpose of marketing is irrelevant: Each person comes to the program and course with his or her own plans, and these need not even concern the acquisition of marketing expertise as such.
sentences.translation: 另一种态度则认为，市场营销的目的无关紧要：每个人来读这个项目和课程都各有自己的打算，这些打算甚至不必与掌握市场营销技能本身有关。

structure.text: The other attitude
is
that the purpose of marketing is irrelevant
Each person comes to the program and course with his or her own plans
and these need not even concern the acquisition of marketing expertise as such

difficult_points: that 引导表语从句，说明这种态度的具体内容。
these 指代前面提到的 his or her own plans（每个人各自的打算）。
as such 意为“本身，就其本身而言”，强调市场营销技能的获取本身。

sentences.sentence: My proposal, which I believe would also be Kant's, is that neither of these attitudes captures the significance of the end to the means for marketing.
sentences.translation: 我的看法——我相信康德也会认同——是：这两种态度都没有把握住“目的之于手段”对市场营销的重要意义。

structure.text: My proposal
which I believe would also be Kant's
is
that neither of these attitudes captures the significance of the end to the means for marketing

difficult_points: which 引导的非限定性定语从句插在主谓之间，评论整个 proposal。
that 引导表语从句，说明 proposal 的具体内容。
neither of 后接复数名词，表示“两者都不”，是全部否定。
the significance of the end to the means 指“目的相对于手段的重要性”，to 表示“相对于”。

sentences.sentence: A field of knowledge or a professional endeavor is defined by both the means and the end; hence both deserve scrutiny.
sentences.translation: 一个知识领域或一项专业工作，是由手段和目的共同界定的；因此，两者都值得仔细审视。

structure.text: A field of knowledge or a professional endeavor
is defined by both the means and the end
hence both deserve scrutiny

difficult_points: be defined by both A and B 表示“由 A 和 B 共同界定”。
hence 表结果，引出“两者都应受到审视”的结论。

sentences.sentence: Students need to study both how to achieve X, and also what X is.
sentences.translation: 学生们既需要学习如何实现目标X，也需要弄清楚目标X究竟是什么。

structure.text: Students
need to study
both how to achieve X, and also what X is
how to achieve X
what X is

difficult_points: both...and also... 强调两件事都需要兼顾。
X 是作者借用的代数符号，泛指“某个目标”，呼应前文手段与目的的讨论。
how to achieve X 与 what X is 分别对应“手段”与“目的”这两个概念。

sentences.sentence: It is at this point that 'Arson for Profit' becomes supremely relevant.
sentences.translation: 正是在这一点上，“以纵火牟利”这门课变得极为重要了。

structure.text: It is at this point
that 'Arson for Profit' becomes supremely relevant

difficult_points: It is...that... 是强调句型，突出强调 at this point（正是在这一点上）。
supremely relevant 是最高程度副词修饰形容词，意为“极其相关/切题”。

sentences.sentence: That course is presumably all about means: how to detect and prosecute criminal activity.
sentences.translation: 那门课程大概完全是关于手段的：如何侦查和起诉犯罪活动。

structure.text: That course
is presumably all about means
how to detect and prosecute criminal activity

difficult_points: presumably 是插入的推测性副词，表示“据推测，大概”。
冒号后内容具体说明 means（手段）指的是什么。

sentences.sentence: It is therefore assumed that the end is good in an ethical sense.
sentences.translation: 因此，人们想当然地认为，这门课的目的从伦理角度看是好的。

structure.text: It is therefore assumed
that the end is good in an ethical sense

difficult_points: It is assumed that... 是被动语态，it 是形式主语，真正的内容在 that 从句中。
in an ethical sense 限定 good 的角度，即“从伦理意义上说”。

sentences.sentence: When I ask fire science students to articulate the end, or purpose, of their field, they eventually generalize to something like, 'The safety and welfare of society,' which seems right.
sentences.translation: 当我请火灾科学专业的学生说清楚他们这一领域的目的（或宗旨）时，他们最终大都会归纳出类似“社会的安全与福祉”这样的答案——这似乎也没错。

structure.text: When I ask fire science students to articulate the end, or purpose, of their field
to articulate the end, or purpose, of their field
they eventually generalize to something like, 'The safety and welfare of society,'
which seems right

difficult_points: or purpose 是插入的同义词，进一步解释 end 的含义。
generalize to something like... 意为“归纳/概括出类似……的答案”。
which 引导非限定性定语从句，指代前面整个引述内容，评论其合理性。

sentences.sentence: As we have seen, someone could use the very same knowledge of means to achieve a much less noble end, such as personal profit via destructive, dangerous, reckless activity.
sentences.translation: 正如我们已经看到的那样，有人可能利用同样的手段知识，去实现一个远不那么高尚的目的，比如通过破坏性、危险且鲁莽的行为谋取个人利益。

structure.text: As we have seen
someone could use the very same knowledge of means
to achieve a much less noble end
such as personal profit via destructive, dangerous, reckless activity

difficult_points: As we have seen 引导方式状语从句，回顾前文已论证过的内容。
the very same 强调“完全相同的”，very 用于加强 same 的语气。
such as 引出具体例子，说明“不那么高尚的目的”所指为何。

sentences.sentence: But we would not call that firefighting.
sentences.translation: 但我们不会把这种行为称为消防工作。

structure.text: But
we would not call that firefighting

difficult_points: would not call 是委婉/虚拟语气，表示“不应当被称作”。
that 指代前一句“利用消防手段谋取私利、造成破坏”的行为。

sentences.sentence: We have a separate word for it: arson.
sentences.translation: 我们有一个专门的词来称呼它：纵火。

structure.text: We have a separate word for it
arson

difficult_points: it 指代上一句中“利用消防手段谋取私利、造成破坏”的行为。
冒号后的 arson 具体说明这个“专门的词”是什么。

sentences.sentence: Similarly, if you employed the 'principles of marketing' in an unprincipled way, you would not be doing marketing.
sentences.translation: 同样地，如果你以不讲原则的方式运用“市场营销原理”，那你做的就不是市场营销了。

structure.text: Similarly
if you employed the 'principles of marketing' in an unprincipled way
you would not be doing marketing

difficult_points: if 引导虚拟条件句，employed 与 would not be doing 构成与现在事实相反的虚拟语气。
in an unprincipled way 与前文 principled 相对，形成呼应对比。

sentences.sentence: We have another term for it: fraud.
sentences.translation: 我们有另一个词来称呼它：欺诈。

structure.text: We have another term for it
fraud

difficult_points: it 指代上一句“以不讲原则的方式运用营销原理”这一行为。
冒号后的 fraud 是对 another term 的具体说明。

sentences.sentence: Kant gives the example of a doctor and a poisoner, who use the identical knowledge to achieve their divergent ends.
sentences.translation: 康德举了医生和投毒者的例子——他们运用完全相同的知识，去实现截然不同的目的。

structure.text: Kant
gives the example of a doctor and a poisoner
who use the identical knowledge to achieve their divergent ends
to achieve their divergent ends

difficult_points: who 引导非限定性定语从句，同时修饰 a doctor and a poisoner 两者。
the identical knowledge 强调知识本身完全一致，为下文对比目的的不同做铺垫。
divergent ends 与前文反复出现的 end（目的）相呼应，指“不同的目的”。

sentences.sentence: We would say that one is practicing medicine, the other, murder.
sentences.translation: 我们会说，其中一个是在行医，另一个则是在行凶杀人。

structure.text: We would say
that one is practicing medicine, the other, murder
the other, murder

difficult_points: one... the other... 对应前一句提到的医生和投毒者两者。
the other, murder 省略了与前半句平行的谓语 is practicing，属于承前省略。
